Job Description: Greetings from WIPRO, We are hiring for multiple roles at Chennai location. Below are the open roles: Campaign Management - Senior Campaign Mngr/ Ops Coordinator Inventory & Analysis - Sales Planning Executive Pricing & Planning - Executive Local Sales Executive (BDRs) / National Sales Executive (sales coordinator) Data & Innovation - Digital Ad Ops Executive Data & Innovation - Strategic I & A Executive (Ad sales research) Marketing Solutions Manager / Executive Position summary: 1. Campaign Management - Senior Campaign Mngr/ Ops Coordinator: The Campaign Manager, Advertising Operations is responsible for managing advertising campaigns from contract execution through campaign completion. This role serves as the central point of coordination across Sales, Sales Planning, Inventory, Legal, Finance, Accounting, Production, and external clients and partners to ensure accurate contract processing, compliant campaign execution, and exceptional client service. The role requires strong attention to detail, sound judgment, and the ability to manage complex workflows involving contracts, media logistics, third-party partnerships, and reporting. The successful candidate thrives in a fast-paced environment and takes ownership of campaign stewardship end to end. 2. Inventory & Analysis - Sales Planning Executive: The Sales Planning Account Manager (Sales Enablement & Support Analyst) plays a critical role in supporting sales execution, inventory planning, and revenue integrity by maintaining accurate attendance, box office, inventory, and delivery data across internal systems. This role partners closely with Sales Planning, Inventory, Campaign Management, Finance, and Sales teams to ensure forecasts, pacing, posts, billing, and makegood processes are supported with timely and accurate data. The successful candidate is highly detail-oriented, analytical, and comfortable working across multiple data sources and systems in a deadline-driven environment. 3. Pricing & Planning - Executive: The Pricing & Planning Executive is responsible for pricing governance, inventory planning, proposal development, and revenue integrity across national, local, programmatic, sponsorship, barter, and investment advertising deals. This role serves as a central partner to Sales, Research, Finance, Campaign Management, Marketing, and Programmatic teams to ensure proposals are feasible, priced appropriately, approved correctly, and executed in alignment with inventory availability and revenue objectives. This position operates in a high-volume, seasonal environment and plays a critical role in protecting downstream revenue recognition, measurement feasibility, and inventory yield. The successful candidate combines strong analytical skills with operational rigor, cross-functional collaboration, and sound business judgment. 4. Local Sales Executive (BDRs) / National Sales Executive (sales coordinator: The Sales Operations & Account Support Executive supports both National and Local Sales teams by managing CRM workflows, RFP intake, proposal and order processing, campaign delivery coordination, and day-to-day sales enablement activities. This role ensures operational excellence across the sales lifecyclefrom lead intake and proposal development through campaign delivery support, billing coordination, and post-campaign reconciliation. This position partners closely with Account Directors, Inside Sales, Planning, Inventory, Finance, and Campaign Management teams. The ideal candidate is highly organized, detail-oriented, service-focused, and comfortable supporting both enterprise-level sales motions and high-volume local and self-serve sales channels 5. Data & Innovation - Digital Ad Ops Executive: The Digital Ad Operations role is responsible for end-to-end execution and stewardship of digital advertising campaigns across Locality / AI Digital platforms, including trafficking, QA, launch, delivery monitoring, pacing management, reporting, and close-out reconciliation. The role also supports programmatic operations and deal execution across SSP/DSP partners where applicable (PG, PMP, and open exchange), acting as an operational escalation point for complex delivery, auction, and configuration issues. This position partners closely with Sales, Account Management, Planning, Finance, and external agencies/vendors to ensure campaigns launch on time, deliver in full, and are measured and reported accurately. 6. Data & Innovation - Strategic I & A Executive (Ad sales research): The Ad Sales Research role supports revenue generation by translating audience, performance, and competitive intelligence into compelling, data-backed sales narratives and actionable insights. This position partners with National and Regional/Local Sales, Marketing Solutions, Pricing & Planning, Inventory, and third-party measurement providers to design measurement studies, deliver analyses, build sales-ready POVs and case studies, and maintain dashboards and recurring reporting. The successful candidate is analytical and detail-oriented, can manage competing requests and deadlines, and is able to communicate complex research findings in clear, client-friendly language. 7. Marketing Solutions Manager / Executive The Marketing Solutions manager is a highly visible role on the ad sales marketing team, which is focused on supporting revenue generation. We are seeking a creative, dynamic, detail-oriented individual with very strong verbal and written communication skills and a proven ability to develop impactful ideas and bring them to life. This person will be responsible for the strategic development and maintenance of sales and marketing materials as well as creating custom solutions for advertisers, responding to RFPs, and handling requests from account directors. A component of this role includes ideating and executing experiential and custom branded content programs as part of our bespoke marketing solutions. The successful candidate is a highly organized multi-tasker who is adept at cross-functional collaboration and excels in a fast-paced environment. The role is a balance of strategic thinking and tactical execution, requiring creative ideas, punchy writing, proactivity, and a positive, go-getter attitude. This person will work hand in hand with sales, planning, research, and the fully integrated marketing and creative team. The role requires experience working with media/ad sales teams and the media/marketing industry at large. A Geographic Information System (GIS) is a computer system that analyzes and displays geographically referenced information. Geographic information system(s), GIS GIS is a technology that is used to create, manage, analyze, and map all types of data. GIS connects data to a map, integrating location data (where things are) with all types of descriptive information (what things are like there). This provides a foundation for mapping and analysis that is used in science and almost every industry. GIS helps users understand patterns, relationships, and geographic context. The benefits include improved communication, efficiency, management, and decision-making. The Process Box Expert is responsible for the end-to-end design, implementation, and optimization of business workflows. You will act as a "Black Box" architecttaking complex, messy inputs and turning them into streamlined, predictable, and automated outputs. Your goal is to maximize operational efficiency through data-driven insights and process automation. Key Responsibilities 1. Process Architecture & Design Mapping: Document "As-Is" and "To-Be" processes using standard methodologies (Lean, Six Sigma, or Agile). Gap Analysis: Identify bottlenecks, manual redundancies, and technical debt within existing workflows. Solutioning: Design high-level "Process Boxes" (modular workflows) that can be easily scaled across different departments. 2. Automation & Technical Integration Platform Mastery: Configure and maintain the organizations process management tools (e.g., SAP, Nintex, Monday.com, or proprietary Process Box software). System Integration: Work with IT to ensure seamless data flow between the process engine and external databases (ERPs, CRMs, etc.). Low-Code Development: Build automated triggers, approval gates, and notification systems to reduce human intervention. 3. Performance Governance KPI Management: Define and monitor Key Performance Indicators to measure process health and ROI. Auditing: Conduct regular "Process Health Checks" to ensure compliance with industry standards and internal SOPs. Reporting: Create visualization dashboards to provide stakeholders with real-time transparency into workflow status. 4. Change Management & Training Mentorship: Act as the Subject Matter Expert (SME) for team members, training them on new digital flows. Stakeholder Alignment: Facilitate workshops to reconcile differing departmental needs into a unified process.

Designation: Virtual Care-Officer Customer Care Position description:Customer Interaction & Query/Complaints Management Job Role includes, handling of inbound calls received from customers enquiring details of the product holdings, product or process related information. (for any / all products areas like Credit Cards, Bank A/Cs, Deposits, Loans, Depositories, Mutual Funds, Debit Cards, Prepaid & Forex Cards or any other Direct Banking related calls) Enquiries & transactions related to the products availed by the customer. Clarifications & queries on billing, payment confirmation, charges, EMI details, statement of account etc. Handling requests such as Debit / Credit Card Pin, NetBanking Password, PIN / IPIN statement of account, Fixed Deposit advice etc. Handling of financial transactions (currently Fixed Deposit opening, Demand Draft/ Managers Cheque requests, Funds transfer request as requested by customers.) Responding to customers with the resolution within the defined TATs. Process the requests as per defined process, while ensuring adherence to the customer authentication process. Answering customer calls within the specified call answer time. Adherence to the defined service delivery standards. Accurate data capture of the request details (eg Hotlisting and Other transactions) as instructed by the customer. Process adherence in respect of submission of the relative annexure to the supervisor for authentication / further action. Accurate and complete data capture of the customer issues / complaints in the relative formats. Logging of the complaints in the system, as per the laid down process. Proper follow-up with the concerned department / branches for resolution and closure of the complaints. Accurate hotlisting of the Debit/ Credit/ Prepaid cards, basis the card / account details provided by the customer o Prompt hotlisting of the card as per process.o Logging the reissuance request basis per customer instruction, as per defined process. Attempting to delight the customer by striking a relationship and fulfilling needs. Call back to customers in case of any unresolved queries etc. Appropriately escalating cases to the Supervisor. Call Quality: Deliver service quality in order to achieve Quality benchmarks defined from time to time. First Call Resolution – 90%( for non-Complaints related calls) Achieve defined Call Quality scores. Achieve defined KAT Scores Achieve defined E-process Scores Audit & Process Compliance: Ensure adherence to process and Audit requirements. Ensure accurate and timely submission of financial transactions & requests. Ensure Error-free logging/recording of customer requests Adherence to set processes of updating customer interactions in CRMNext & ensuring timely updations of interactions in appropriate systems. Schedule Adherence & Service Productivity standards: • Consistently meet the service productivity standards – Staff needs to meet the service productivity standards as defined from time to time.• Ensure adherence to schedule and login times as applicable – Staff needs to maintain service adherence Other Operational Activities Maintenance of records / record keeping. Keep self updated on the product/ process knowledge as per the training imparted by the Training / Supervisors/ Quality. Shift Adherence Nil Unscheduled offs Appropriate attendance record updation Nil Exception of ID maintenance Adherence to Audit and compliance process as defined. Primary Responsibilities: Responsible for ensuring delivery of competent and consistent service in line with set standards that results in an enhanced customer experience for bank customers. To ensure that the defined productivity targets on service levels, call handling time , quality parameters are met consistently To ensure zero defects processing of all customer requests, including prompt and accurate Hotlisting of lost cards reported. To ensure complete and accurate resolution within the defined TATs. To ensure process compliance as per the set Audit and SQ guidelines.

Technical Lead Application Security Location: Mumbai, India (Onsite) Experience Required: 20+ years (Application security + AI/ML security) Compensation: As per industry benchmarks Employment Type: Full-Time | Permanent Role Overview We are hiring a Techno Managerial Lead – Application Security to join our CISO team in Mumbai. This is a critical, hands-on leadership role that blends strategic oversight with deep technical expertise in application security. The ideal candidate will spearhead security testing of applications, evangelize secure software development practices, lead secure code reviews, and collaborate across teams to embed security into the software development lifecycle (SDLC). Key Responsibilities Strategic Leadership: Define and evolve the bank’s application security strategy and roadmap including AI/ML and LLM security testing. Align security initiatives with business goals and regulatory requirements. Technical Execution: Lead secure SDLC integration across development teams(including DevSecOps) of security testing tools. Oversee application security tools & processes for SAST, SCA DAST, Code Review across diverse technologies such as Web, Mobile, API etc. Participate and provide expert opinion in secure architecture design reviews for critical applications. Ensure periodic refresh of test cases catalogue against bank specific use-cases, emerging threats & global frameworks. Define, publish, and govern policies, secure coding standards, and open-source usage guidelines. Leverage AI security tools for scanning, fuzzing, and penetration testing of AI models. Apply best practices from OWASP Top 10 for ML/LLMs, MITRE ATLAS, NIST AI RMF, and ISO/IEC 42001 to test AI/ML assets. Stay informed about emerging threats and security trends in AI/ML technologies, and provide recommendations for enhancing security posture. Ensure AI model security testing framework aligns with internal policy, national regulatory requirements, and global best practices. Classification - Internal Classification - Internal Plan and execute security tests for AI/LLM systems, including jailbreaking, RAG hardening, Program Management: Build and lead a high-performing AppSec team for pre-golive security testing as well as post-go live testing of scoped applications through structured calendar program. Develop and track KPIs and metrics to measure program effectiveness. Manage vendor relationships and maintain centralized governance across application security, source code review, open source, and AI Security programs. Drive compliance with internal and regulatory requirements through periodic security testing and reporting. Stakeholder Engagement: Collaborate with engineering, risk, development, DevOps, risk, and compliance teams. Provide executive-level reporting and risk insights. Build and expand a security-first development culture through continuous secure coding training, workshops, and security champion’s programme to promote security awareness and advocacy within development teams Required Skills & Experience 20 years of experience in cybersecurity, with at least 15 years in application security program management, development & testing (SAST/DAST/SCA) and minimum 5 years in leadership roles. Proven track record of managing large-scale AppSec programs in BFSI or regulated environments. Experience in dealing with regulatory bodies and response. Hands-on experience with secure coding, manual application penetration testing, and DevSecOps practices. Experience working with cloud-native applications and microservices architectures. Deep understanding of OWASP Top 10 for Web, Mobile and API and their corresponding OWASP Testing guides, CWE and OWASP developer guide and other secure coding standards. Proficiency in security tools: Fortify, Checkmarx, Veracode, MobSF, Frida, Xposed Framework, Cydia, JDgui, Burp Suite, etc. Strong programming background (Java, .NET, Python, etc.) would be an added advantage. Familiarity with CI/CD pipelines and integrating security into DevOps. Hands-on experience with AI/ML security or secure MLOps/LLMOps Proficient in Python, TensorFlow/PyTorch, HuggingFace, LangChain, and common data science libraries Strong understanding of AI-specific threat models (MITRE ATLAS) and security benchmarks (OWASP Top 10 for ML/LLMs) Excellent communication, leadership, and stakeholder management skills. Ability to translate technical risks into business impact clearly to non-technical stakeholders Classification - Internal Classification - Internal Qualifications and Certifications Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Information Security, or related field. Certifications: CISA, CISM, CISSP, CSSLP, OSCP, OSWA, OSWE, SANS WAPT SEC542, Cloud Security, ML Security, or relevant AI/ML certificates Why Join Us? Work on mission-critical security initiatives in a high-impact role. Be part of a forward-thinking cybersecurity team in a leading financial institution. Opportunity to shape the future of secure banking applications.

Job Purpose The Senior Auditor will lead audits of the Retail Liability portfolio, which includes Savings Accounts, Corporate Salary Accounts, Current Accounts, Term Deposits, Government Deposits, and other related products. The role ensures compliance with regulatory requirements, internal policies, and industry best practices, while assessing internal controls and identifying process gaps to mitigate operational, compliance, and fraud risks. Job Responsibilities Actionables Audit Planning & Execution Conduct end-to-end audits for liability products (Savings, Current, Corporate Salary, Term Deposits, Government Deposits, etc.)., including scoping, fieldwork, and reporting. Review processes related to account opening, KYC compliance, interest calculation, charges, customer servicing and master data maintenance in Core Banking System Risk Assessment & Control Evaluation Identify key risks across liability operations, including fraud, operational lapses, and regulatory non-compliance. Evaluate internal controls and recommend enhancements to strengthen risk management. Regulatory Compliance Ensure adherence to RBI guidelines, Banking Regulation Act, and internal compliance standards. Validate compliance with AML/CFT norms, KYC requirements, and customer due diligence processes. Review of master maintenance in core banking & satellite systems is in compliance with regulatory guidelines Data Analytics & Sampling Use data analytics tools to identify anomalies, trends, and potential risk indicators. Perform sample testing of transactions and account records for accuracy and completeness. Reporting & Follow-up Prepare detailed audit reports highlighting findings, root causes, and actionable recommendations. Track implementation of audit recommendations and report status to senior management. Educational Qualifications Key Skills CA / MBA (Finance) / Certified Internal Auditor (CIA) Strong understanding of retail liability products and related processes. Knowledge of RBI regulations, KYC/AML norms, and banking compliance frameworks. Proficiency in audit methodologies, risk assessment, and internal control evaluation. Analytical skills with experience in data analysis tools. Excellent communication and report-writing skills. Ability to work independently and manage multiple audits simultaneously. Experience Required Candidates with experience in handling audits / retail liability operations would be mandatory Work Experience 7 10 years

Job Title: Unit Head - Internal Audit - Digital Journeys
Job Description: Job Purpose: To lead and manage internal audits of digital customer journeys across onboarding, servicing, and transaction-related processes, ensuring that end-to-end controls are effectively designed and operating as intended. The role focuses on functional aspects of digital channels control embedment, compliance with regulatory requirements, process efficiency, evaluating customer experience within the Bank’s digital ecosystem. Job Responsibilities Actionables Audit Planning and Risk Assessment Develop and execute risk-based audit plans covering digital journeys for customers, including onboarding, account servicing, and customer support journeys. Identify and assess key risks associated with account opening, KYC, authentication, and service fulfillment. Evaluate the control environment governing customer lifecycle management on digital platforms. Collaborate with business and operations teams to understand product workflows, customer touchpoints, and back-end integration. Audit Execution Lead audits of digital processes such as: Customer Onboarding Journeys: CASA account opening, e-KYC, video KYC, CKYC integration, document validation. Account Servicing Journeys Customer Transaction Journeys: Fund transfers, bill payments, standing instructions, auto-debit mandates. Customer Support Journeys: Chatbots, service requests through app/web, grievance redressal, closure of requests. Verify control embedment across customer touchpoints — authentication, authorization, exception handling, and escalation. Ensure adherence to RBI KYC Master Direction, digital onboarding circulars, and internal policies. Review interface between digital channels and core banking / CRM systems from a functional control perspective. Maintain complete documentation of testing, evidence, and results in line with audit methodology. Reporting and Issue Management Draft high-quality audit reports highlighting control weaknesses, process gaps, and compliance issues. Recommend pragmatic control improvements and service efficiency measures. Present findings to senior management and business heads, ensuring clarity and actionable outcomes. Track open audit issues, validate corrective actions, and ensure timely closure. Coordination and Governance Work closely with teams from Digital Banking, Operations, Compliance, and Risk to ensure comprehensive audit coverage. Support thematic and cross-functional reviews on customer experience, grievance management, and digital process controls. Provide inputs on control design for new or revised customer journeys and digital products. People and Process Leadership Supervise a team of auditors, review their work, and ensure adherence to audit quality standards. Guide team members in understanding evolving digital processes and regulatory expectations. Foster continuous improvement, collaboration, and high professional standards within the team. Educational Qualifications Key Skills Essential: Chartered Accountant (CA) Preferred: CIA / CISA / CRISC Digital / Data Certifications (Value-add) Certification in UX/UI evaluation, Design Thinking, or CX analysis / Data Analytics certifications (e.g., Google Analytics, Tableau, Power BI) / Lean Six Sigma (for process efficiency assessment) Strong understanding of products, customer servicing processes, and digital banking channels. Sound knowledge of RBI KYC / AML guidelines, digital onboarding regulations, and account servicing controls. Ability to evaluate functional controls and customer journey design from an operational and risk perspective. Analytical and problem-solving skills with attention to control design and compliance linkage. Strong communication, stakeholder management, and reporting skills. Ability to manage multiple audits concurrently and drive result-oriented reviews Experience Required: 12 – 15 years of experience in internal audit, digital audit, risk management for digital channels, or process control within a large Indian bank or financial institution Minimum 5 years of experience in leading or supervising audits of central / support functions. Key Performance Indicators : Timely and high-quality completion of audit assignments. Quality and impact of audit findings and recommendations. Timely closure and sustainability of corrective actions. Positive feedback from auditees and management. Team development, adherence to methodology, and professional conduct.

Job Purpose: To lead and supervise internal audits of branches to ensure adherence to regulatory requirements, internal policies, and control standards. The role is responsible for the effective execution, review, and quality assurance of branch audits covering operational, compliance, and customer service aspects. The position also ensures timely and accurate reporting of findings, closure of control gaps, and continual improvement in branch operations and risk culture. Job Responsibilities Actionables Audit Execution and Supervision Lead and oversee internal audits of retail branches, wholesale banking branches & currency chests assigned as per the approved audit plan. Ensure comprehensive coverage of key operational and compliance areas, including: Liability operations: customer onboarding, KYC/AML adherence, documentation checks. Cash, vault, and ATM management, including reconciliation and control processes. Locker operations, customer service, and regulatory compliance checks. Clearing, CMS, Depository, SLI loans, home loans, agri loans & loan against gold jewelry related processes Supervise field auditors and ensure adequacy of sampling, evidence collection, and documentation. Conduct review meetings with branch officials to clarify audit queries and resolve discrepancies during fieldwork. Keep oversight on branches & verticals in the allocated geographical area. Review & Reporting Review draft audit reports for accuracy, consistency, and objectivity of observations. Classify audit findings based on risk severity and ensure recommendations are practical and actionable. Conduct exit meetings with branch and regional management to communicate key findings and agree on corrective actions. Ensure timely submission of finalized audit reports and working papers to the zonal/corporate audit office. Issue Follow-Up and Closure Track and monitor open audit issues across assigned branches. Validate corrective actions and provide confirmation for issue closure in line with internal audit policy. Escalate delays or repeated non-compliance cases for management review. Support analysis of root causes for recurring deficiencies and recommend process improvements. Governance, Quality and Coordination Ensure adherence to internal audit methodology, documentation standards, and reporting protocols. Review working papers prepared by auditors for adequacy and quality assurance. Coordinate with Compliance, Risk, and Operations teams for cross-functional issue resolution. Support thematic, investigative, or special audits assigned. People and Team Leadership Supervise and guide a team of branch auditors across multiple locations. Provide functional training and on-the-job support to team members. Review team performance and ensure uniform application of audit methodology and rating criteria. Promote professional conduct, independence, and continuous learning within the team. Educational Qualifications Key Skills Essential: Chartered Accountant (CA) / MBA (Finance or Operations) / Certified Internal Auditor (CIA). Preferred: Certification in Banking Audit, Risk, or Compliance (e.g., CISA, CRMA). In-depth understanding of branch banking operations, compliance, and control frameworks. Sound knowledge of RBI guidelines, KYC/AML norms, and audit standards. Strong ability to identify process lapses, control deficiencies, and operational risks. Excellent analytical, interpersonal, and communication skills. Proven team leadership and coaching abilities. High degree of integrity, confidentiality, and attention to detail. Experience Required 10 12 years of experience in internal audit or branch operations within a large private sector bank or financial institution. At least 5 years in a supervisory role managing branch or field audits. Exposure to risk-based audit methodology and audit automation tools preferred. Key Performance Indicators : Timely and complete execution of assigned branch audits. Quality and accuracy of audit findings and reports. Timely follow-up and closure of open issues. Reduction in repeat or high-severity findings. Team performance, discipline, and adherence to audit standards.

Job Purpose: The Senior Auditor will execute audits of internal and office accounts for a large banking organization. This role ensures compliance with regulatory guidelines (including RBIs RBIA framework), internal policies, and industry best practices. Participate in internal training, peer learning, and knowledge-sharing sessions. Strong communication and report-writing abilities. High integrity and professional ethics. Ability to carry out audits independently Good analytical ability to interpret field findings and identify trends or anomalies. Working knowledge of Excel / Power BI / dashboard tools for data analysis preferred. Integrity, objectivity, and adherence to confidentiality requirements. Experience Required 5 – 7 years of experience in internal audit within banking or financial services. Strong knowledge of RBI guidelines, banking regulations, and internal control frameworks Proficiency in audit tools, ERP systems, and MS Office.

Essential Services : Role & Location fungibility At ICICI Bank, we believe in serving our customers beyond our role definition, product boundaries, and domain limitations through our philosophy of customer 360-degree. In essence, this captures our belief in serving the entire banking needs of our customers as One Bank, One Team . To achieve this, employees at ICICI Bank are expected to be role and location-fungible with the understanding that Banking is an essential service . The role descriptions give you an overview of the responsibilities, it is only directional and guiding in nature. About the program ICICI Business Leadership Programme ICICI Business Leadership Programme is offered in partnership with NISM (an institute established by SEBI). The programme offers Post Graduate Certificate in Securities Market (PGCSM). The programme duration is of 1 year and it is offered at Patalganga campus of NISM. This specialized and integrated programme focuses on providing conceptual grounding, skill building and industry exposure. ICICI Bank has partnered with NISM to jointly offer One-year PGCSM which aims to create professionals such as Solution Managers in domestic sales team of Treasury, dealers, and Institutional Sales Professionals in the securities markets. It would be a client relationship management role involving extensive engagement with clients. The programme offers a blend of classroom and hands-on learning. The curriculum is aligned to cater to the new age skills of the industry. On successful completion the participants are on boarded to ICICI Bank at Manager-I grade. Eligibility Education: Graduates, Engineers, Post-Graduate/MBA 2025 pass-outs are eligible to apply. Qualification: Minimum with 60% in 10th and Graduation Role and Location Fungibility: Applicant should be locationally mobile and willing to do sales Experience: 3 to 8 years Program Structure Program Duration: Term I, II & III comprises of 9 months of classroom training and Term IV consists of internship at ICICI Bank for 3 months . Remuneration Stipend during the program: Term I, II & III: Stipend of Rs. 10,000 per month Term IV: Stipend of Rs. 50,000 per month Starting Salary (CTC): 13.00 Lakh to 14.00 Lakh Program Fees The program fee is 4 lakhs plus applicable GST . This fee must be paid to the training partner . Selection Process Online Aptitude Test: Once the applicants are shortlisted, the first step is to undergo an online aptitude test. It comprises of five sections (Verbal, Quantitative, Logical ability, General Awareness, Video based questions). Interview (Face-to-Face): Applicants who have successfully completed the aptitude test shall be invited for the in-person interview. Detailed information on the interview will be shared on your registered mail ID. We are currently inviting applications from applicants across India ICICI Business Leadership Program

About the Role A Relationship Manager - Farmer Finance is a core anchor in taking our banking solutions and services to our valuable customers. This involves a 360-degree banking approach. The Relationship Managers should have an inclination to understand the customer needs and offer solutions in line with the needs. The core philosophy to hold is being Fair to the Customer and Fair to the Bank”. At ICICI Bank, our customer-centric relationship managers are responsible for acquiring new customer relationships and increasing the wallet share of existing customer relationships with a resolute focus on service quality and customer delight. They collaborate and work with different internal teams, thereby offering the best in class customer service. In line with our philosophy of taking the entire bank to the customers, our relationship managers work on the whole suite of offerings like saving solutions, loan products, overdraft solutions, Insta loans, investment solutions, care products, trade solutions, business loans, or wealth management solutions while upholding our fundamental value of being Fair to the Bank and Fair to the Customer . Bankable individuals not just Bankers: We hire from across industries! We at ICICI Bank are in continuous pursuit of creating consistent customer delight and superior experience. Our Relationship Managers are empowered individuals who champion the cause of customer experience and have penchant for building and nurturing business relationships. If you are an individual, whose heart echoes with our aspiration, look no more. We do not just choose diverse minds but celebrate their success. If you are a bankable individual without banking experience, you are very much welcome, irrespective of your present core responsibilities or the industry associated. Our training programs equip you to get ready for the role of a Relationship Manager. Professionals from across industries are welcome to apply for the role of Relationship Manager. Key Responsibilities Customer Service Create service excellence by partnering with customers through their life cycle and offering suitable products and services based on their financial needs while being fair to the customer and fair to the bank in all engagements. Business Development Responsible for sourcing and selling Kisan Credit Cards(KCC), increasing the wallet share of existing customers, and acquiring new customers by offering relevant agricultural products. Champion 360-degree banking Offer products based on customer needs in collaboration with other internal teams in the bank for suitable product offerings. About the program The ICICI Bank Aspire Program nurtures young professionals with "Seva Bhav" - values of humility, adaptability, and a zeal to learn. Focused on relationship management, it emphasizes empathy, professionalism, and providing banking solutions to customers. The program includes extensive residential training, followed by nationwide placements in relationship management (sales) roles. The Aspire Program will prepare selected applicants for Relationship Manager (RM) job, which is a sales role. This role will involve client interactions and will require travel within and around the assigned location/city. Selected applicants shall be placed in any location based on the requirements of the Bank and will undergo a rigorous training by ICICI Bank . Eligibility Education: Graduates, Engineers, Post-Graduate/MBA 2025 pass-outs are eligible to apply. Qualification: Minimum with 60% in 10th and Graduation Role and Location Fungibility: Applicant should be locationally mobile and willing to do sales Experience: 0 to 6 months. Program Structure Program Duration: 2 Months Classroom Training Remuneration Stipend during the program: 18,000 per month. Starting Salary (CTC): 4.50 Lakh to 5.00 Lakh (Based on location). Selection Process Online Aptitude Test: Once the applicants are shortlisted, the first step is to undergo an online aptitude test. It comprises of five sections (Verbal, Quantitative, Logical ability, General Awareness, Video based questions). Interview (Face-to-Face): Applicants who have successfully completed the aptitude test shall be invited for the in-person interview. Detailed information on the interview will be shared on your registered mail ID. We are currently inviting applications from applicants across India

About the Role: As a Data Warehouse Architect, you will be responsible for managing and enhancing data warehouse that manages large volume of customer-life cycle data flowing in from various applications within guardrails of risk and compliance. You will be managing the day-to-day operations of data warehouse i.e. Vertica. In this role responsibility, you will manage a team of data warehouse engineers to develop data modelling, designing ETL data pipeline, issue management, upgrades, performance fine-tuning, migration, governance and security framework of the data warehouse. This role enables the Bank to maintain huge data sets in a structured manner that is amenable for data intelligence. The data warehouse supports numerous information systems used by various business groups to derive insights. As a natural progression, the data warehouses will be gradually migrated to Data Lake enabling better analytical advantage. The role holder will also be responsible for guiding the team towards this migration. Key Responsibilities: Data Pipeline Design: Responsible for designing and developing ETL data pipelines that can help in organising large volumes of data. Use of data warehousing technologies to ensure that the data warehouse is efficient, scalable, and secure. Issue Management : Responsible for ensuring that the data warehouse is running smoothly. Monitor system performance, diagnose and troubleshoot issues, and make necessary changes to optimize system performance. Collaboration: Collaborate with cross-functional teams to implement upgrades, migrations and continuous improvements. Data Integration and Processing : Responsible for processing, cleaning, and integrating large data sets from various sources to ensure that the data is accurate, complete, and consistent. Data Modelling : Responsible for designing and implementing data modelling solutions to ensure that the organizations data is properly structured and organized for analysis. Key Qualifications & Skills: Education Qualification: B.E./B. Tech. in Computer Science, Information Technology or equivalent domain with 10 to 12 years of experience and at least 5 years or relevant work experience in Datawarehouse/mining/BI/MIS. Experience in Data Warehousing: Knowledge on ETL and data technologies and outline future vision in OLTP, OLAP (Oracle / MSSQL). Data Modelling, Data Analysis and Visualization experience (Analytical tools experience like Power BI / SAS / ClickView / Tableu etc). Good to have exposure to Azure Cloud Data platform services like COSMOS, Azure Data Lake, Azure Synapse, and Azure Data factory. support root-cause analysis and driving resolution through structured coordination with onshore teams and clients. • Own AR aging reviews and support client teams with detailed AR analysis to enable collection prioritization and dispute resolution. • Ensure all billing and cash application activities comply with client policies, internal audit requirements, and documented SOPs. • Act as the primary escalation point for AR-related operational issues, system challenges, and client concerns, ensuring timely resolution and clear communication. • Support internal and external audits by providing billing, cash application, and reconciliation documentation, explanations, and walk-throughs as required. • Identify opportunities for process standardization, efficiency improvement, and automation within billing and cash application workflows, and support implementation initiatives. The Marketing Development Team consisting of Business Development Representatives (BDR) is responsible for creating pipeline for Sales. Our BDRs play an important part in driving revenue by helping to set meetings and qualify leads into opportunities. This position works closely with Sales and Marketing to help create interest for our new product offerings and services. Role & responsibilities : Business Development creates the great first impression to potential customers who are committed to enhancing a better customer experience for their own brand. Your responsibilities will include but not limited to: Lead Generation: You will proactively identify, contact, and cultivate new business opportunities using a combination of cold calling, emailing, and social channels to leverage meetings where you uncover customer experience opportunities. Sales Cycle Management: Managing the entire sales process, from initial contact and qualification to demo, negotiation, and closing. Prospecting: Use prospecting strategies to lead initial outreach to prospects in various C-level positions Customer Relationship Management (CRM) : Be able to quickly build report with prospects while identifying the needs & challenges of a prospective customer and creating interest in Concentrix services. Research & Analysis : Learn industry and funding signals and incorporate business trends in your prospect outreach. Job Title: Support Engineer III, Just Walk Out Tech
Job Description: As a Support Engineer III in the Technical Operations Center (TOC), you are a technical leader and force multiplier for the team. You go beyond resolving incidents you own the patterns behind them, drive the initiatives that eliminate them, and raise the bar for how the team operates. You will lead oncall response for complex, high-severity incidents, own deep dive investigations end-to-end, and partner with Dev teams to drive automation and lasting fixes. On non-oncall shifts, you lead reduction initiatives, mentor peers, and shape the processes that make the team stronger. Key job responsibilities - Lead oncall response for JWO store incidents as the primary responder, owning triage, escalation, and resolution for high-severity, complex incidents within SLA windows - Own deep dive investigations end-to-end identifying systemic failure patterns, driving root cause analyses, and partnering with Dev teams to validate that fixes hold - Manage rotational shift coverage as part of a globally distributed follow-the-sun model, including weekend rotations, ensuring seamless handoffs and shift health documentation - Drive zero-touch resolution and automation initiatives identifying manual intervention patterns, proposing runbook candidates, and seeing them through to implementation - Partner with Dev and engineering teams during escalations, providing structured reproduction steps, impact assessments, and technical recommendations - Own SOP development and knowledge base quality identifying gaps, authoring updates, and ensuring the teams operational documentation reflects current best practices - Mentor SE1s and SE2s through oncall shadowing, deep dive reviews, and day-to-day coaching - Lead team-level operational reviews, presenting data-backed findings and driving follow-through on action items About the team - Diverse Experiences Amazon values diverse backgrounds. Whether your career is just starting or has followed a non-traditional path, we encourage you to apply. - Why AWS AWS is the worlds most comprehensive cloud platform, trusted by startups and Global 500 companies alike to power their businesses. - Work/Life Balance We value work-life harmony and strive for flexibility, so our people can thrive both at work and at home. - Inclusive Team Culture Employee-led affinity groups and inclusion events foster collaboration and empower our people to bring bold, fresh perspectives. - Mentorship and Career Growth Endless knowledge-sharing, mentorship, and career-advancing resources to help you grow into a better-rounded professional. - 2+ years of software development, or 2+ years of technical support experience - Experience scripting in modern program languages - Experience troubleshooting and debugging technical systems - Knowledge of web services, distributed systems, and web application development - Experience with REST web services, XML, JSON

Job Title: Senior Quality Auditor, Artificial General Intelligence
Job Description: The Senior Quality Auditor serves as a specialist who becomes the organizations deepest expert on 1-2 complex machine learning data workflows while maintaining hands-on audit execution. This role within the Machine Learning Data Operations job family leads Quality Audit Design (QuAD) teams for high-complexity workflows, handles the most ambiguous audit scenarios, and generates workflow-specific quality analysis that drives customer insights and workflow optimization. Key job responsibilities Workflow Expertise Development - Become the organizations deepest expert on 1-2 assigned complex workflows through intensive pilot participation and continuous learning - Participate in workflow pilots as an annotator to build foundational knowledge before production begins - Develop comprehensive understanding of workflow conventions, edge cases, and quality requirements Team Leadership - Lead QuAD teams of 3-5 Quality Auditors for Template 2 (High-Engagement Support) workflows - Provide day-to-day leadership and make real-time quality decisions during iteration phases - Handle escalations from team members and serve as the go-to expert for workflow-specific questions - Mentor and develop Quality Auditor team members through coaching, feedback, and knowledge sharing Audit Execution - Execute audits on the most ambiguous and complex scenarios, reducing escalations to Quality Specialists - Maintain 60-70% hands-on audit execution to preserve expertise required for real-time decision-making - Validate team audit decisions and ensure consistency across QuAD team members - Support Template 1 workflows by auditing high-complexity scenarios requiring specialized judgment Analysis & Insights - Generate workflow-specific deep dives and thematic analysis providing insights beyond conventions adherence scores - Identify error patterns, convention evolution, and areas of ambiguity within assigned workflows - Provide actionable recommendations for workflow optimization and quality improvement - Partner with DSLs and customers on workflow-specific quality insights Strategic Collaboration - Provide input to audit strategy design led by Quality Specialist I - Collaborate with Quality Specialists on cross-workflow patterns and tactical improvements - Participate in workflow design discussions with customers and stakeholders - Support new-tool onboarding for operationalized and bootstrapped interfaces - Track record of generating actionable insights from quality data. - Bachelors degree or equivalent - 2+ years of equivalent experience - Experience working with machine learning data workflows or similar technical domains - Hands-on experience in leadership and mentoring - Strategic thinking about workflow optimization and process improvement - Effective stakeholder communication skills with the ability to influence decision-makers and customers - Domain expertise in STEM, language, or technical domains highly valued. - Experience with explainable machine learning and artificial intelligence methodologies and tools - Experience in leadership roles, overseeing teams conducting data analysis, process improvements, creative problem solving, and cross-functional collaboration - Experience using data and metrics to drive actionable insights at scale - Experience that includes strong analytical skills, attention to detail, and effective communication abilities - Experience handling administrative details independently, while exercising good judgment in keeping team members adequately informed - Familiarity with audit methodologies and quality frameworks - Experience participating in pilot phases and workflow development.

Job Title: Business Analyst, Middle Mile Capacity Planning
Job Description: We are looking for a good fit to join our Transportation Long Term Capacity Planning Team, part of the larger Supply Chain Team. This team plays a crucial role in strategically planning and executing Amazons Transportation network topology over a 1-3 year horizon. As a Business Analyst, you will be at the forefront of uniting cross-functional teams to evolve our network, continuously optimizing for cost, speed, and reliability while maintaining a strong customer focus. In this role, you will be responsible for supporting with data and developing tools for capacity solutions that address both current and future demands, ensuring business continuity even through peak periods. Your work will directly contribute to our long-term strategy programs, preparing Amazons logistics network for sustained growth while striking the delicate balance between delivery speed, operational efficiency, and cost-effectiveness. You will leverage your expertise in supporting for long-term forecasting and capacity planning to make data-driven decisions that shape the future of our Middle Mile operations. This position requires a strategic mindset, strong analytical skills, and the ability to collaborate effectively with diverse teams across the organization. Your insights and recommendations will be instrumental in guiding senior leadership decisions and driving the continuous improvement of our vast logistics network. You are expected to have exceptional analytical, planning, and written/verbal communication skills. You know and love working with business intelligence tools, are comfortable accessing and working with big data from multiple sources, and are enthusiastic about partnering with the business to deliver results. You are a self-starter, comfortable with ambiguity, able to think big, and enjoy working in a fast-paced environment. Join our team and help shape the future of Amazons logistics network, contributing to our mission of becoming Earths most customer-centric company while balancing delivery speed with efficiency and cost-effectiveness! Job Title: Senior Quality Auditor, Artificial General Intelligence
Job Description: The Senior Quality Auditor serves as a specialist who becomes the organizations deepest expert on 1-2 complex machine learning data workflows while maintaining hands-on audit execution. This role within the Machine Learning Data Operations job family leads Quality Audit Design (QuAD) teams for high-complexity workflows, handles the most ambiguous audit scenarios, and generates workflow-specific quality analysis that drives customer insights and workflow optimization. Key job responsibilities Workflow Expertise Development - Become the organizations deepest expert on 1-2 assigned complex workflows through intensive pilot participation and continuous learning - Participate in workflow pilots as an annotator to build foundational knowledge before production begins - Develop comprehensive understanding of workflow conventions, edge cases, and quality requirements Team Leadership - Lead QuAD teams of 3-5 Quality Auditors for Template 2 (High-Engagement Support) workflows - Provide day-to-day leadership and make real-time quality decisions during iteration phases - Handle escalations from team members and serve as the go-to expert for workflow-specific questions - Mentor and develop Quality Auditor team members through coaching, feedback, and knowledge sharing Audit Execution - Execute audits on the most ambiguous and complex scenarios, reducing escalations to Quality Specialists - Maintain 60-70% hands-on audit execution to preserve expertise required for real-time decision-making - Validate team audit decisions and ensure consistency across QuAD team members - Support Template 1 workflows by auditing high-complexity scenarios requiring specialized judgment Analysis & Insights - Generate workflow-specific deep dives and thematic analysis providing insights beyond conventions adherence scores - Identify error patterns, convention evolution, and areas of ambiguity within assigned workflows - Provide actionable recommendations for workflow optimization and quality improvement - Partner with DSLs and customers on workflow-specific quality insights Strategic Collaboration - Provide input to audit strategy design led by Quality Specialist I - Collaborate with Quality Specialists on cross-workflow patterns and tactical improvements - Participate in workflow design discussions with customers and stakeholders - Support new-tool onboarding for operationalized and bootstrapped interfaces - Track record of generating actionable insights from quality data. - Bachelors degree or equivalent - 2+ years of equivalent experience - Experience working with machine learning data workflows or similar technical domains - Hands-on experience in leadership and mentoring - Strategic thinking about workflow optimization and process improvement - Effective stakeholder communication skills with the ability to influence decision-makers and customers - Domain expertise in STEM, language, or technical domains highly valued. - Experience with explainable machine learning and artificial intelligence methodologies and tools - Experience in leadership roles, overseeing teams conducting data analysis, process improvements, creative problem solving, and cross-functional collaboration - Experience using data and metrics to drive actionable insights at scale - Experience that includes strong analytical skills, attention to detail, and effective communication abilities - Experience handling administrative details independently, while exercising good judgment in keeping team members adequately informed - Familiarity with audit methodologies and quality frameworks - Experience participating in pilot phases and workflow development.

The Senior Business Analyst will join the high-visibility, growth-oriented Finance Operations, Global Data & Analytics (GDA) team. Role Overview We are looking for a hybrid Business Analyst cum Program Manager to spearhead the management and evolution of our IoT-based Energy Monitoring System. Technical & Professional Requirements Domain Expertise: Strong understanding of IoT Architecture (Sensors, Gateways, MQTT/LoRaWAN) and Electrical Systems (kWh, Power Factor, Demand). Analysis Tools: Strong Excel skills Project Tools: Working knowledge to project management tools Education: B.E./B.Tech in Electrical, Electronics, or Systems Engineering; MBA or specialized Business Analysis training is a plus. Experience: 5–8 years of professional experience, with at least 2 years in IoT or Energy Management.

Job Title: VOIS Vodafone - Microsoft Azure Cloud Security Architect (9-14 yrs)
Job Description: About this Role : We are seeking a Secure by Design professional to lead technically orientated security assessments for Office IT (OIT) services within Global Cyber Security. The individual will ensure that products, services and infrastructure across Vodafone are secure by design, assist in defining and reviewing security requirements, and provide expert guidance to programme and project teams. This role is essential in enabling the business to implement secure technologies, reduce risk effectively, and ensure compliance with global cyber security standards. What you will do : - Deliver technically focused security assessments for OIT products and services, ensuring alignment to secure by design principles. - Define, communicate and oversee implementation of security requirements for new and existing services. - Provide expert security design consultancy to architecture, delivery and operational teams across markets and group entities. - Evaluate risks and compliance status of Office IT products, cloud services and AI-driven platforms, ensuring mitigation to acceptable levels. - Guide suppliers and third parties in meeting Vodafones security expectations and standards. - Support agile teams by coaching Security Champions and managing external security consultancy resources. - Influence a collaborative approach that positions security as a business enabler. Who you are : - Extensive professional experience in information technology and cyber security, with strong capability in securing Office IT products and services. - Demonstrated expertise with Microsoft technologies, including O365 Security & Compliance, Copilot, Microsoft Defender, Azure and cloud productivity ecosystems. - Strong understanding of AI security and governance, including LLM risk management, prompt security and secure enterprise AI deployment.Solid background in enterprise risk management across cloud, Office IT and AI-related environments. - Proficient in agile ways of working, modern cloud service consumption models and identity management in collaboration ecosystems. - Ability to communicate complex technical matters to diverse non-technical business stakeholders clearly and effectively. - Holds relevant qualifications such as CISSP, CISM, CISA, CRISC, ISO 27001 LA, GIAC, TOGAF or SABSA. - Minimum 5 years of experience in cyber security, 10+ years with Microsoft Office 365 and related services, and 5+ years in cloud security. Not a Perfect Fit? Concerned you may not meet every requirement? Vodafone is committed to creating an inclusive workplace where everyone can thrive. If you are excited about this role but your experience does not align exactly with every aspect of the job dscription, you are encouraged to apply. You may be the right candidate for this or another opportunity, and the recruitment team will support you in exploring where your skills fit best. What's in it for you : - Opportunity to influence global cyber security practices across multiple markets and functions. - Work on cutting-edge technologies including cloud, AI and enterprise collaboration platforms. - Exposure to cross-functional global teams and high-impact security governance. - Ability to shape secure solutions for products used by millions globally. What skills you will learn : - Advanced secure-by-design methodologies across enterprise-scale environments. - Deep practical understanding of AI security governance and LLM risk management. - Enhanced ability to communicate security strategy to senior business stakeholders. - Practical experience in multi-market risk mitigation and supplier security oversight. - Broader leadership capability through coaching and influencing technical teams.

Job Description: Quality & Service Engineering Role Overview: The DGM – Quality & Service Engineering leads a nationwide function that governs product quality , service performance , and customer experiencedriven reliability improvements across both manufacturing partners (OEM/EMS) and authorized service & repair networks. This role is responsible for strategic quality governance, crossfunctional coordination, driving supplier excellence, and leading a technical team that ensures bestinclass quality throughout the product lifecycle—from NPI to end-of-life service. Key Responsibilities: 1. Quality Strategy & Governance - Define and implement the quality strategy, standards, and governance framework. - Own enterpriselevel quality metrics: DPPM, DOA, RMA, field reliability, and warranty cost. - Lead continuous improvement programs (Lean/Six Sigma). - Present monthly and quarterly quality reviews to senior leadership. 2. Factory Quality Leadership (OEM/EMS Partners) - Oversee quality performance across mass production partners. - Conduct executivelevel quality reviews with EMS/OEM leadership. - Govern NPI builds, process validation, and manufacturing test strategies. - Approve and validate CAPA for systemic issues. 3. Service Center Quality & Customer Experience - Lead nationwide service network quality. - Establish service quality frameworks: MTTR, FTFR, repeat repair, CSAT. - Oversee field failure analysis and repair analytics. - Drive closure of systemic product issues. 4. Product Reliability & DataDriven Decision Making - Own reliability programs and field performance modeling. - Publish monthly Quality Dashboards and scorecards. - Benchmark supplier and servicepartner performance. 5. Leadership, Team Management & Stakeholder Engagement - Lead a team of Quality Engineers and Field Quality Managers. - Build organizational capability through training and certification. - Manage escalations and crossfunctional decision-making. Key Skills & Competencies: - Prior experience in managing teams who drive supplier quality responsibilities for factories or service centres is highly desirable, particularly relating to mobile phones, set-top boxes, routers, and gateways . - Strong understanding of electronics/telecom device manufacturing. - Expertise in NPI, DFM, DFS, reliability engineering. - Mastery of Six Sigma, 8D, FMEA, SPC, RCA. - Excellent stakeholder and communication skills. - Data-driven mindset with operational rigor. Qualifications: - B.E./B.Tech in Electronics, Electrical, Telecom, or related field. - 15- 16 years in Quality, Reliability, or Service Engineering. - Experience managing teams and largescale programs. - Certifications in Lean, Six Sigma preferred.

Job Title: Service Engineer ( Quality) - Field & Factory Support
Job Description: Heres a Job Description (JD) tailored for a Quality Service Engineer (Field / Factory Support) with 6–10 years of experience , who oversees quality assurance and service performance across manufacturing plants and service centres. Job Title: Quality Service Engineer – Field & Factory Support Role Overview: The Quality Service Engineer is responsible for ensuring consistent product and service quality across manufacturing partners, repair centres, and customer service networks. The role involves on-site visits to mass production facilities (e.g., EMS partners manufacturing consumer devices) and authorized service centres to monitor processes, analyse failure trends, drive improvements, and ensure adherence to quality and reliability standards. Key Responsibilities: 1. Factory Quality Support Conduct regular audits and inspections at OEM/EMS production sites. Monitor critical manufacturing processes to ensure adherence to established quality standards, specifications, and regulatory requirements . Verify adherence to quality control plans, SOPs, and IPC/ISO standards. Collaborate with the manufacturing team to identify root causes of high-volume defects (DPPM) or process variations and implement Corrective and Preventive Actions (CAPA) . Analyze production data, test reports, and yield rates to detect trends, anticipate quality risks, and drive process optimization . Review incoming inspection, in-process inspection, and final inspection reports. Analyze production test data (ICT, FCT, RF, etc.) and identify yield or reliability issues. Support New Product Introduction (NPI) builds and provide early feedback on design for manufacturability and serviceability. Work with R&D, procurement, and vendor quality teams to resolve line issues. 2. Service Center Quality Monitoring Perform periodic visits and audits to authorized service centers to evaluate their technical proficiency, adherence to repair procedures, and efficiency in customer resolution. Analyze field failure data, returned parts (RMAs), and service center reports to identify top failure modes and emerging product reliability issues. Facilitate Root Cause Analysis (RCA) for critical field failures, collaborating with Product Engineering, Design, and Manufacturing teams to implement permanent fixes. Develop and distribute technical service bulletins, training materials, and updated repair procedures to service centers based on failure trends. Track and report on key service quality metrics, such as Mean Time To Repair (MTTR) , First Time Fix Rate (FTFR) , and customer satisfaction (CSAT) related to service. 3. Data Analysis & Reporting Compile monthly quality reports covering factory yield, field failures, DOA rate, and warranty trends . Generate CAPA (Corrective & Preventive Action) reports and follow up on closure. Support cost reduction through quality improvement and rework minimization. Key Skills & Competencies: Strong knowledge of quality management systems (e.g., ISO 9001) and manufacturing control processes. Strong understanding of consumer electronics manufacturing (PCBA, plastic molding, assembly, testing). Knowledge of ISO 9001 / ISO 14001 / TL9000 systems. Experience in root cause analysis, 8D methodology, FMEA, SPC, and DOE . Prior experience in managing supplier quality responsibilities for factories or service centres is highly desirable, particularly relating to mobile phones, set-top boxes, routers, and gateways. Familiarity with hardware reliability, RF testing, and repair operations . Hands-on experience with field failure data analysis and yield improvement . Good communication and reporting skills with cross-functional coordination ability. Willingness and ability to travel frequently (domestically) to manufacturing sites and service centres. Qualifications: B.E. / B.Tech in Electronics, Electrical, Telecom, or related discipline. 6–10 years of experience in Quality / Service / Reliability Engineering in consumer electronics, telecom equipment, or IoT products. Certification in Six Sigma / Lean / Quality Tools preferred.

Main Tasks Physical verification of Gold Packets, Cash including Vault Cash and Foreign Currency as to its correctness with the system records. Checking of ornaments as to its quantity, weight, quality and purity taken for pledge during the current gold inspection period. Checking of greater than 5 months gold packets as to its quantity, weight, quality and purity for which gold inspections were done earlier. Verification of Gold Loan pledge/release documents and reporting of anomalies if any observed. Verifying whether the ornaments pledged under all schemes are solid ornaments and LP/SP ornaments are not accepted for pledge under any schemes. Verification and reporting of any overdue gold loans, Low Purity and Spurious accounts are re-pledged or re-pledged under higher rate. Verification of Documents and ornaments/coins for Swarnavarsham/Shwethavarsham Loans disbursed. Verification of all Gold Loan related products/Packets, MSME Packets, Mahilamitra Loan Packets and Chitty Security Packets etc. Conducting Handing over/Taking over of branch as and when required by the Management and sending reports of the same to BAI Dept. Purity Index updation in the system Verification and sealing of auction related packets. The Site Engineer Infrastructure and Administration is responsible for managing and supervising infrastructure projects, renovations, and maintenance activities across branches and facilities of the organization. This role involves ensuring the quality and timely execution of construction and repair works, maintaining hygiene and operational standards, and coordinating with internal teams, landlords, vendors, and contractors.The Site Engineer will monitor site progress and ensure compliance with regulatory and organizational standards. KEY RESPONSIBILITIES Infrastructure Requirements Serve as the technical lead on all infrastructure-related projects for MFL and group companies. Oversee periodic repair and renovation work in existing branches and group company facilities. Conduct regular branch visits to assess maintenance needs and address hygiene requirements. Coordinate with vendors and Head Office (HO) to execute maintenance and repair tasks efficiently. Work closely with Zonal Infrastructure Managers to resolve complaints related to Complain Management Services (CMS) & other infrastructure concerns. Identify and manage local vendors to provide support for repairs and maintenance work. Oversee vendor contracts to ensure quality service delivery. Supervise and coordinate the execution of ongoing and upcoming construction, renovation, and infrastructure improvement projects and ensure all projects are completed on time, within budget, and adhere to quality standards. Identify and address electrical, plumbing, and carpentry issues with local vendor support. Branch Expansion, Shifting and Consolidation Collaborate closely with Zonal Infra Managers to address requirements for new branch setups, shifting, mergers, renovations, and ongoing maintenance. Prepare or review branch layouts, align with operational needs, share with landlords for consent, and vet contractor site plans before HO approval. Liaise with landlords to ensure incorporation of their scope of work in new branch projects. Supervise the construction of Strong Rooms as per RBI specifications and organizational standards. Coordinate with Strong Room Door vendors and contractors to ensure timely and accurate installation of Strong Room doors. Oversee all civil works, plumbing, interior and exterior finishing, structured cabling, electrical installations, and safety measures (e.g., mesh/grill protection). Ensure all works align with approved plans, Bills of Quantities (BOQ), and quality standards. Ensure smooth and efficient handover of merger or shifting sites to landlords (LLs), addressing all concerns and completing agreed-upon requirements. Cost, Quality and Quantity control, Billing Ensure materials used meet the quality standards specified in the Work Order. Obtain prior approval from field teams and the Head Office for additional works outside the scope of the Work Order. Conduct joint measurements with contractors to verify work progress and accuracy. Monitor and supervise site work for group companies as required, ensuring timely and efficient project completion. Coordination and reporting Effectively coordinate with field teams to support activities in existing branches, new locations, and merger sites. Prepare and share regular work progress reports with relevant stakeholders. Arrange Handing Over Certificates (HOC) from landlords for completed projects. Obtain No Objection Certificates (NOC) from landlords for merger sites. Travel pan-India as required to address urgent project needs or exigencies. KEY INTERACTIONS Internal Stakeholders Procurement Field IT Operations Zonal Infra Team Business Team External Stakeholders Landlords Vendors Statutory authorities KEY ROLE DIMENSIONS This is a role with responsibilities for overseeing infrastructure and administration operations within the assigned region. KEY CHALLENGES Managing infrastructure and maintenance needs for a branch network larger than the industry average Handling branches in widely spread-out and remote locations, which adds complexity to logistics and supervision. Reliance on local vendors for immediate support, leading to potential variability in quality and response times. Lack of centralized Repair & Maintenance (R&M) and housekeeping service agency contracts, resulting in inconsistent service levels and additional coordination efforts. KEY DECISION Choosing and coordinating with local vendors for immediate support in small-scale repairs and maintenance tasks. Prioritizing and scheduling site visits, monitoring progress, and addressing bottlenecks to ensure timely completion. Vetting and recommending site layouts and plans for approval by the Head Office. Addressing site-level issues, including coordination with landlords for handover certificates (HOC) and no-objection certificates (NOC). Deciding on resource allocation and collaboration with Zonal Infra Managers and field teams for seamless project execution. Ensuring all works adhere to regulatory guidelines, such as RBI specifications for strong room construction and other safety standards. KEY SKILLS & BEHAVIOURAL ATTRIBUTES Negotiation and Relationship Management Vendor and Stakeholder Coordination Technical Expertise Attention to Detail Effective Communication Collaboration and Teamwork Accountability and Customer Focus

ROLE SUMMARY The Head -Credit will be the strategic leader responsible for overseeing the credit risk management framework across multiple verticals of the NBFC. With an AUM of 50,000 crore, the Head Credit will ensure robust credit policies, portfolio diversification, and risk mitigation strategies to safeguard asset quality while enabling sustainable growth. This role is essentially the guardian of asset quality and the architect of credit growth strategy in a high-scale NBFC. KEY RESPONSIBILITIES Credit Strategy & Policy Develop and implement enterprise-wide credit risk policies aligned with regulatory guidelines (RBI, SEBI, etc.). Establish sector-specific credit frameworks for diverse verticals (Retail, SME, Corporate, Infrastructure, Housing Finance, Vehicle Finance, etc.). Oversee underwriting standards, scorecards, rating models, and collateral valuation frameworks Drive innovation in credit assessment models, leveraging data analytics, AI/ML, and alternative credit scoring. Ensure disciplined credit appraisal, sanctioning, and documentation processes Portfolio Quality & Risk Monitoring Monitor portfolio quality across verticals, ensuring NPA levels remain within acceptable thresholds. Design early warning systems and stress-testing mechanisms for macroeconomic and sectoral risks . Oversee credit risk appetite and exposure limits across geographies and business lines. Partner with collections, legal, and recovery teams to improve resolution outcomes Leadership & Governance Lead a team of credit risk professionals across verticals, fostering a culture of accountability and performance. Collaborate with the Board, Audit Committee, and Risk Committee to present credit risk reports and portfolio insights. Ensure compliance with internal controls, regulatory requirements, and external audits Operational Excellence Streamline credit approval processes to balance speed with risk prudence. Implement digital credit platforms for faster decision-making and improved customer experience. Drive automation in underwriting, monitoring, and collections Stakeholder Management Work closely with business heads to align credit policies with growth objectives. Engage with regulators, rating agencies, and investors to build confidence in the NBFCs credit practices. Represent the NBFC in industry forums and policy discussions on credit risk. KEY INTERACTIONS Internal Stakeholders External Stakeholders Board of Directors and Board Committees (Risk Management Committee, Audit Committee) CEO / Executive Leadership Team Business Heads Retail, SME, Corporate, Infrastructure, Housing Finance, Vehicle Finance Risk Management, Compliance, Legal, and Internal Audit Collections, Recovery, and Operations Teams Finance, Treasury, and ALM IT, Digital, Analytics, and Data Science Teams People & Culture for capability building and talent management Reserve Bank of India (RBI), NHB, and other regulators Statutory Auditors, Internal Auditors, Concurrent Auditors Credit Rating Agencies Banks, Financial Institutions, and Lenders Investors and Trustees (where applicable) Valuers, Legal Counsel, and External Risk Consultants Technology and Analytics Partners KEY ROLE DIMENSIONS Enterprise Scope: Pan-India responsibility across multiple lending verticals and customer segments Portfolio Complexity: Secured and unsecured products, short- and long-tenure loans, retail to large-ticket exposures Decision Authority: Credit policy ownership, approval authority as per delegated powers, final escalation authority KEY SKILLS & BEHAVIOURAL ATTRIBUTES Strategic thinking with ability to balance growth and risk. Deep expertise in credit underwriting, portfolio management, and risk analytics. Strong leadership, communication, and stakeholder management skills. Tech-savvy with exposure to digital lending, fintech partnerships, and advanced risk modeling. Behavioral Attributes- Driven and in alignment with our Purpose “Transforming the life of the common man by improving their financial well-being” and anchored by our core value of integrity, collaboration, and excellence. EDUCATION / EXPERIENCE Education: CA/MBA/FRM/CFA or equivalent in Finance, Risk Management, or related fields. Experience: 15+ years in credit risk management, with at least 5 years in a leadership role in a large NBFC/Bank. Proven track record of managing multi-vertical credit portfolios exceeding 50,000 crore. Strong understanding of regulatory frameworks, capital adequacy norms, and risk-based pricing

L&G America seeks a Middle Office Operations Analyst specializing in Trade Settlement Support to provide operational expertise across multiple platforms (SWIFT, CRIMS, DTCC CTM, and Alert) supporting the Index Trading Desk. Provide periodic metrics and control summaries for management review Required Qualification: 3-5 years experience with settlement related activity and trade life-cycle in global markets. Strong communication to be able to articulate issues clearly, either verbally or written, across all levels Experience in trade support, risk management, or operations within an asset management environment. Knowledge of investment guidelines and regulatory compliance. Excellent organizational, problem-solving, analytical skills. Job Title: Territory Head - Hdfc Life - Virtual Floor - VRM
Job Description: What are we looking for? Territory Head (TH) position in HDFC Life is a Second Level Leadership (SLL) position that is expected to achieve the Organizational goals for the assigned territory in terms of revenue and cost, develop team members (Corporate Sales Manager (CSM/Sr. CSM), who is a First Level Leader - FLL) and duly maintain an engaging relationship with the channel partners of the mapped geography. What does the job entails? Maintaining professional engagement levels consistently with the Channel Partner Leadership Teams (including Branch Head, Cluster Head, Circle Head, TPP team, etc.,) leading to minimal escalations and ensures service resolution within TAT and achieves business objectives. Accountable to drive Business Sales KPIs pertaining to Top-line, Market share, Product Mix, UpSell, SLG and 13th month Persistency and Control Attrition which means the TH is also accountable to drive overall persistency, threshold level market share and a profitable product mix (Term share) for the mapped Territory. Plan the Business Travel for the month/quarter and ensure the same is been fulfilled to engage team and drive business objectives. Develop the CSM/Sr. CSM skills (First Level Leadership - FLL) and ensure they manage their direct reportees (FLS) with apt/right engagements leading to low attrition and high productivity levels in the Territory. Invest time and efforts towards developing the FLL skills and competencies to ensure they manage their FLS fraternity efficiently and thereby leading to higher channel partner engagement and reduced attrition. Build ones own team through Referrals and ensure 100% manning overall for the month Accountable to ensure the 0-6 months FLS engagements are been done timely and aptly by the FLLs leading to FLSs on time probation success (includes conducting timely On-the-Job training support by FLL). Ability to manage the multi-task activities pertaining to Sales Management Process, First Time Right on Login to Conversion, Org. initiatives, Contests, Personal & Business Hygiene, etc., are been efficiently driven and sustained in the Territory. Maintain collaborative approach with internal and external stakeholders and thereby fulfill business objectives seamlessly
